purpose: Assesses structural resilience of the expansion by tracking the private sector financial balance (saving minus investment) using the accounting identity that all sectoral balances sum to zero.
components: Private sector financial balance (currently surplus 2-3% GDP)
External balance / current account (currently deficit 3-3.5% GDP)
Historical comparison to prior cycle peaks
current_assessment: Private sector surplus of 2-3% GDP indicates structurally greater resilience than 1999-2000 (deficit ~5%) or 2005-2007 (deficit ~3-4%). The Godley framework correctly predicted both 2001 and 2008 from private deficit readings. Rated as the single most valuable analytical contribution across all agents.
limitations: Only one agent developed this explicitly despite the accounting being verifiable
Does not reveal distribution of surplus across household income deciles
The surplus mechanically depends on the fiscal deficit — not independent
Behavioral responses to surplus erosion are uncertain
